farmInputs.source: Industry reporting / al-Monitor / Trading Economics — April 2026
farmInputs.context: Urea prices at AUD $1,350-$1,420/tonne, up ~60% since the Hormuz closure. Bank of America estimates 65-70% of global urea supply is at risk. Australia imports the majority of its fertiliser (urea, DAP, MAP) — primarily from the Middle East and Asia, through the same shipping routes now blocked. Australian growers are shifting to less fertiliser-intensive crops for winter planting. This is a double hit for agriculture: fuel costs up AND input costs up simultaneously. Downstream effect: food prices will follow with a 3-6 month lag.
